page: Microsoft Azure EC8eds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ECEDV5 server family. Powered by an Intel processor with x86_64 architecture, this instance features 8 dedicated virtual CPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The virtualization layer is managed by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This server configuration includes 232 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk attachments. It does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. There are no onboard graphics processing units, making it a CPU-only compute resource.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the EC8eds_v5 offers a high density of memory relative to its processing cores, making it a resource-efficient option for workloads that are memory-bound rather than compute-bound. This hardware profile is suited for relational database management systems, in-memory caching layers, data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring a large memory footprint without the overhead of additional CPU cores.
description: Microsoft Azure EC8eds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the ECEDV5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance is equipped with 232 GB of local storage and supports premium storage configurations, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, the server provides a cost-effective balance of high memory density relative to CPU resources, minimizing unnecessary compute overhead. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive workloads, including relational databases, in-memory caches, and enterprise applications.
